tables.description: Organizational units within the airport, grouping employees under functional areas such as operations, maintenance, or administration.

columns.description: Unique identifier for the department

tables.description: Master record of all airport staff members, capturing their identity and department affiliation.

columns.description: Unique identifier for the employee
Full name of the employee

tables.description: Reference list of job roles or position types that can be assigned to employees.

tables.description: Junction table mapping employees to the roles they fulfill, supporting many-to-many relationships.

Foreign key referencing the employee
Foreign key referencing the role

tables.description: Reference table defining named work shifts with their start and end times.

Time when the shift begins

tables.description: Planned shift assignments for employees on specific calendar dates.

Date on which the shift is scheduled

tables.description: Requests submitted by employees for time off, capturing leave type, duration, and approval status.

columns.description: Unique identifier for the leave request

Category or type of leave requested
First day of the leave period

tables.description: History of training programs completed by employees with completion dates.

columns.description: Unique identifier for the training record

Date when the training was completed

tables.description: Reference classification for equipment categories used across airport operations.

columns.description: Unique identifier for the equipment type
Name or category of the equipment type

tables.description: Master inventory of physical equipment assets with their current status and type.

columns.description: Unique identifier for equipment item
Name of the equipment item
Current operational status of the equipment

tables.description: Transactional records tracking which employee has been issued equipment and when it was returned.

columns.description: Unique identifier for equipment assignment record
Foreign key reference to assigned equipment
Foreign key reference to assigned employee
Date when equipment was assigned to employee
Date when equipment was returned by employee

Detailed description of the maintenance issue

tables.description: Audit trail of completed maintenance activities performed on equipment.

columns.description: Unique identifier for maintenance history record

Date when maintenance was performed
Additional remarks and notes about maintenance

columns.description: Unique identifier for aircraft stand

tables.description: Transactional records of which gate is allocated to which flight and at what time.

Foreign key reference to assigned flight
Timestamp when gate was assigned to flight

tables.description: Transactional records mapping flights to aircraft parking stands at specific times.

columns.description: Unique identifier for stand assignment
Foreign key reference to assigned stand
Foreign key reference to assigned flight
Timestamp when stand was assigned to flight

tables.description: Core operational record tracking the end-to-end turnaround process for a flight between arrival and departure.

columns.description: Unique identifier for turnaround operation
Foreign key reference to flight being turned around
Timestamp when turnaround operation started

Current status of turnaround operation

columns.description: Unique identifier for the turnaround checklist item
Reference to the parent turnaround process
Description of the turnaround checklist task or step
Boolean flag indicating if the checklist item is completed

columns.description: Unique identifier for the boarding task record

Boolean flag indicating if the gate is ready for boarding
Boolean flag indicating if baggage has been loaded

Amount of fuel currently remaining in the aircraft
Amount of fuel required for the next flight
Amount of fuel added during the refueling operation

columns.description: Unique identifier for the catering task record

Timestamp when the catering service ends

columns.description: Unique identifier for the cleaning task record

Timestamp when the cleaning service ends

tables.description: Historical scan log of baggage movements through various locations in the airport.

tables.description: Records of baggage reported as lost or mishandled, with descriptions and current status.

columns.description: Unique identifier for the lost baggage record
Reference to the lost baggage item
Current status of the lost baggage case
Detailed description of the lost baggage incident

entity_relationships.business_meaning: A Department groups many Employees (1:M). Each Employee belongs to exactly one Department, organizing the workforce into functional units.
An Employee can hold multiple Roles over time via EmployeeRole (M:N), is assigned to one Department, and is scheduled across Shifts through Schedule entries.
Employees receive Equipment via EquipmentAssignment (M:N) and are dispatched to operational tasks such as CleaningTask within a Turnaround (1:M).
Equipment instances are classified by EquipmentType (1:M) and allocated to Employees through EquipmentAssignment, with MaintenanceRequest and MaintenanceHistory tracking lifecycle events (1:M).
A Flight is assigned to one Gate and one Stand per occurrence (M:N via GateAssignment/StandAssignment) and triggers a Turnaround operation (1:M) and baggage movements (1:M).
A Turnaround belongs to one Flight and decomposes into checklists and discrete operational tasks (BoardingTask, RefuelingTask, CateringTask, CleaningTask) each executed by Employees (1:M).
Baggage is associated with a Flight (1:M) and tracked through loading, unloading, scanning events, and a LostBaggage record when it goes missing (1:M each).

business_processes.tables: department, employee, attendance, role, employee_role, shift, schedule, leave_request, training_record
flight, gate, stand, gate_assignment, stand_assignment, turnaround_operation, turnaround_checklist, boarding_task, refueling_task, catering_task, cleaning_task

use_cases.name: Workforce Scheduling & Attendance
use_cases.description: Plan employee shift assignments across departments, record daily attendance, and track leave requests and training records to ensure coverage for airport operations.
use_cases.involved_tables: department, employee, shift, schedule, attendance, leave_request, training_record, role, employee_role

use_cases.name: Flight Turnaround Coordination
use_cases.description: Orchestrate all ground operations (boarding, refueling, catering, cleaning, checklists) tied to a flight turnaround, ensuring every task is dispatched to qualified employees within SLA windows.
use_cases.involved_tables: flight, turnaround_operation, turnaround_checklist, boarding_task, refueling_task, catering_task, cleaning_task, employee, equipment_assignment

use_cases.name: Equipment Lifecycle & Maintenance
use_cases.description: Manage ground support equipment inventory by type, assign equipment to employees and flights, log maintenance requests, and review historical maintenance records to minimize downtime.

use_cases.name: Gate & Stand Resource Allocation
use_cases.description: Allocate airport gates and aircraft stands to incoming and outgoing flights, preventing conflicts and optimizing terminal capacity utilization.

use_cases.name: Baggage Tracking & Lost Baggage Recovery
use_cases.description: Monitor baggage from loading through unloading, capture scan events along the route, and investigate lost baggage incidents linked to specific flights and baggage IDs.
use_cases.involved_tables: baggage, baggage_loading, baggage_unloading, baggage_scan, lost_baggage, flight

sample_queries.question: List all employees in a given department along with their assigned shift schedules.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, d.department_name, s.shift_id, s.start_time, s.end_time
FROM employee e
JOIN department d ON e.department_id = d.department_id
LEFT JOIN schedule sc ON e.employee_id = sc.employee_id
LEFT JOIN shift s ON sc.shift_id = s.shift_id
WHERE d.department_name = 'Ground Operations'
ORDER BY e.employee_id, s.start_time;

sample_queries.question: Find all turnaround tasks for a flight with their current status, grouped by task type.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT t.flight_id, 'Boarding' AS task_type, bt.status
FROM boarding_task bt
JOIN turnaround_operation t ON bt.turnaround_id = t.turnaround_id
WHERE t.flight_id = 'FL1234'
UNION ALL
SELECT t.flight_id, 'Refueling', rt.status
FROM refueling_task rt
JOIN turnaround_operation t ON rt.turnaround_id = t.turnaround_id
WHERE t.flight_id = 'FL1234'
UNION ALL
SELECT t.flight_id, 'Catering', ct.status
FROM catering_task ct
JOIN turnaround_operation t ON ct.turnaround_id = t.turnaround_id
WHERE t.flight_id = 'FL1234'
UNION ALL
SELECT t.flight_id, 'Cleaning', cl.status
FROM cleaning_task cl
JOIN turnaround_operation t ON cl.turnaround_id = t.turnaround_id
WHERE t.flight_id = 'FL1234';

sample_queries.question: Count the number of active employees per department, broken down by role.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT d.department_name, r.role_name, COUNT(DISTINCT e.employee_id) AS employee_count
FROM department d
JOIN employee e ON e.department_id = d.department_id
JOIN employee_role er ON e.employee_id = er.employee_id
JOIN role r ON er.role_id = r.role_id
WHERE e.status = 'Active'
GROUP BY d.department_name, r.role_name
ORDER BY d.department_name, employee_count DESC;

sample_queries.question: Retrieve the full scan history for a lost baggage item, ordered chronologically.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT lb.lost_baggage_id, b.baggage_id, bs.scan_location, bs.scan_time, bs.scan_status
FROM lost_baggage lb
JOIN baggage b ON lb.baggage_id = b.baggage_id
LEFT JOIN baggage_scan bs ON b.baggage_id = bs.baggage_id
WHERE lb.status = 'Open'
ORDER BY b.baggage_id, bs.scan_time;

sample_queries.question: Compute the average turnaround duration per flight to identify turnaround SLA performance.
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT f.flight_id, COUNT(t.turnaround_id) AS turnarounds, AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (t.end_time - t.start_time)) / 60.0) AS avg_minutes
FROM flight f
JOIN turnaround_operation t ON f.flight_id = t.flight_id
WHERE t.end_time IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY f.flight_id
ORDER BY avg_minutes DESC;
